Example: Southwest corner of parking lot, in landscape planter, next to fire hydrant
Is there a transfer hole cover present?
Transfer holes are used on Peak Diversion style systems. In some cases, there may be a cover blocking this hole that must be removed during activation.
Was the transfer cover removed during activation?

The transfer hole cover must be removed for the unit to operate. Please ensure transfer hole cover is removed. If this unit does not utilize a transfer hole, you have answered the above question incorrectly.

Do blank headplates have rubber gaskets around them?
The system CANNOT BE ACTIVATED if the gaskets are not installed on the blank headplates.

Is the downstream confluence structure (if applicable) free from damage and out of bypass mode?

entry_values.text: Miscellaneous (please add description to each photo)

entries.label: The Filterra components have been installed in accordance with Contech Engineered Solutions specifications and recommendations. The tree has been planted, mulch installed, and any items used for shipping have been removed. The system is in new condition, and is considered fully functional and active.

The Jellyfish components have been installed in accordance with Contech Engineered Solutions specifications and recommendations. The filter cartridges have been installed and are in new condition. Cartridge receptacles that are not in use (if applicable) were appropriately capped using solid gaskets headplates and solid lids to prevent unfiltered water from bypassing filtration treatment. The Jellyfish Filter is fully functional and is considered active.
The Filterra has not been activated at this time. The contractor and/or owner shall be notified by way of this report what items require attention prior to activation being successfully completed.

The Jellyfish Filter has not been activated at this time. The contractor and/or owner shall be notified by way of this report what items require attention prior to activation being successfully completed.

Inspection of the Jellyfish Filter is key in determining future maintenance requirements, and to develop a site specific history of pollutant loading characteristics. In general, inspections should be performed a minimum of twice during the first year of operation to assess sediment levels in the sump, floatable pollutant accumulation, and filter tentacle operation.
Would you like to schedule an inspection for this Jellyfish Filter?
Select date and time for inspection and notify the owner, or responsible party, that a follow up inspection has been scheduled.

Please notify the owner or appropriate party that a follow up inspection has not been scheduled. When they are ready to schedule an inspection, Contech has a nationwide network of Certified Maintenance Providers that can contact. For more information, call Contech at 1-800-548-4667, or visit www.conteches.com and search for maintenance services.
